egg-bites-in-the-instant-pot.name: Egg Bites In The Instant Pot®
instructions: Spray egg molds with nonstick cooking spray. Sprinkle crumbled bacon into the molds.

Combine shredded cheese, cottage cheese, half-and-half, eggs, and salt in a blender; process until smooth. Divide mixture equally amongst the molds, filling each about 3/4 full. Cover with foil, wrapping tightly around the edges of the molds.

Pour water into the inner pot of a multi-functional pressure cooker (such as Instant Pot®). Close and lock the lid. Select steam setting according to manufacturer's instructions; set timer for 8 minutes. Allow 5 to 10 minutes for pressure to build.
Release pressure using the natural-release method according to manufacturer's instructions, for 10 minutes. Release remaining pressure carefully using the quick-release method, about 5 minutes.
Hold the mold over a wire rack and gently push egg bites out. Serve immediately or cool and store in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.

egg-candy-semifreddo-teleculinaria.name: Egg Candy Semifreddo

ingredients.item: 100 g of butter at room temperature

instructions: Prepare the cake: beat the butter with eggs and milk in the blender. Add sugar, chocolate, flour and yeast and stir well. Lay in a form, previously greased with butter and sprinkled with flour, and bring it to the oven, preheated at 180°C, for about 30 minutes. Take it back, let it cool down, and then undo it.

Prepare the cream: scrape the gelatin leaves in cold water for a few minutes. Beat the cream in whipped cream, add the condensed milk and then the gelatin, previously drained and melted in the microwave, stirring until it is well dissolved. Reserve.

Prepare the sweet egg: take 100 ml of water to the fire with sugar, until you get pearl point. Dissolve the mostena flour in the undone yolks. Add the yarn syrup carefully and without stopping stirring. Lie on the pot and take it back to the fire, until it thickens a little.

In a glass glass bowl, have some of the cake undone and cover with some of the cream, coffee cookies and some of the egg candy. Repeat the layers, finishing with the egg candy. Take it to the cold until it gets very fresh.

endive-romaine-and-orange-salad-for-two.name: Endive, Romaine, And Orange Salad For Two

ingredients.prep: preferably Cara Cara, cut into segments

ingredients.item: Head of purple or white endive

instructions: Whisk oil, orange juice, vinegar, salt, and 1/2 tsp. pepper in a large bowl. Add orange segments, endive, romaine, and parsley and toss with your hands until well coated. Using your hands, lift salad from bowl and transfer to a platter, leaving excess dressing behind. Top with Gouda and remaining 3/4 tsp. pepper.

ermine-icing.name: Ermine Icing
instructions: Cook milk and flour together in a saucepan over medium-high heat, stirring constantly, until thickened to the consistency of pudding. Bring to a boil, then pour into a heat-proof container. Lay a piece of parchment paper directly on the surface to prevent a skin from forming. Refrigerate for 8 hours to overnight.
Cream sugar, butter, and salt in a stand mixer or using a hand mixer until sugar granules disappear.

Remove boiled milk mixture from the refrigerator and stir with a spatula to ensure there are no lumps. Slowly pour into creamed butter mixture and mix on low speed until completely incorporated. Stop the mixer and scrape down the bowl. Increase the speed to medium and beat until frosting is light and fluffy, 5 to 10 minutes. Beat in vanilla extract.

escaroupim-style-eels-teleculinaria.name: Eels scavenger-style
instructions: Get the eels, cut them in pieces and season them with white wine, salt, pirpiri, blonde leaf and sweet pepper.

Sauce the onions, cut into slices, and chopped garlic teeth, in a pot with the olive oil, stirring from time to time. Add the tomato cubes, pulp and eels, correct the seasonings and the amount of broth and let cook. Remove from the fire and serve.
